articles.title: Urban Air Trampoline Park Settles for $300K After Go-Kart Death of 6-Year-Old
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park in Port St. Lucie, Florida has agreed to pay $300,000 following a state investigation into a go-kart crash that killed a 6-year-old girl. The settlement is a significant liability reminder for FEC operators running motorized ride attractions, particularly go-karts. Operators should review their safety protocols, ride height and age restrictions, and insurance coverage in light of this case.

articles.title: Florida Family Settles Wrongful Death Claim After Child Killed in FEC Go-Kart Crash
articles.summary: A Florida settlement has been reached between the state and the family of a 6-year-old killed in a go-kart crash at an indoor FEC. The case underscores the critical importance of ride safety standards, operator training, and liability preparedness for FEC venues offering motorized attractions. Industry operators should use this as a prompt to audit go-kart safety procedures and guest supervision policies.

articles.title: Six Flags St. Louis Reinstates Chaperone Policy After Mass Brawl on Opening Day
articles.summary: Six Flags St. Louis brought back its chaperone policy requiring guests 16 and under to be accompanied by an adult aged 21 or older after fights involving up to 100 mostly teenage guests broke out on opening day. The policy limits each chaperone to accompanying no more than six minors. FEC operators facing similar crowd-control challenges may find this a useful policy model for managing unsupervised youth on high-traffic days.

articles.title: Dallas Entertainment Group Plans $50M FEC Complex in Downtown Clearwater
articles.summary: A Dallas-based entertainment group is planning a $50 million multi-concept entertainment destination in downtown Clearwater, Florida, anchored by what is being billed as the world's largest movie theater screen. The project signals continued investor appetite for large-format urban entertainment destinations. FEC developers and investors should watch this project as a case study in mixed-use entertainment real estate development.

articles.title: World's Largest Movie Theater Screen Anchors New Clearwater Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: A new entertainment complex planned for downtown Clearwater, Florida will feature the world's largest cinema screen alongside other FEC-style attractions. The project is part of a broader redevelopment of the Cleveland Street corridor. It reflects a growing trend of combining cinema with experiential entertainment to drive longer dwell times and higher per-visit spending.

articles.title: $50M Clearwater Entertainment Destination Has Ties to Church of Scientology
articles.summary: A planned $50 million entertainment destination in downtown Clearwater, Florida is drawing scrutiny due to its ties to the Church of Scientology, which owns significant real estate in the area. The project includes an entertainment complex with a massive cinema screen and other attractions. FEC operators and developers should be aware of the community and reputational dynamics surrounding this high-profile project.

articles.title: ThunderZone Family Entertainment Center Announces Grand Opening Date
articles.summary: ThunderZone Family Entertainment Center has officially announced its opening date, adding a new FEC to its local market. New venue openings are a key indicator of industry growth and competitive activity. Operators in the region should take note as a new competitor enters the market.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Named One of USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has received recognition from USA Today as one of the most trusted brands by parents, a meaningful endorsement for a family entertainment brand rebuilding its image following bankruptcy and rebranding efforts. Trust and brand reputation are increasingly important differentiators in the FEC space as parents make spending decisions. This recognition may bolster Chuck E. Cheese's franchise and marketing efforts.

articles.title: Valleyfair Adds 13 New Water Slides in Major Waterpark Expansion Opening May 23
articles.summary: Valleyfair amusement park in Shakopee, Minnesota is opening a major waterpark expansion on May 23 that adds 13 new slides, bringing the park's total water attractions to 24. The Superior Shores Waterpark expansion is designed to cement Valleyfair's position as Minnesota's top summer destination. Regional FEC and waterpark operators can study this expansion as an example of using new attractions investment to drive competitive differentiation.

articles.title: Six New Wavegarden Surf Parks in Development as Sector Sees Global Boom
articles.summary: Wavegarden has announced six new surf park destinations in development worldwide, citing an unprecedented global boom in the surf park sector. The company highlights its technology's low energy and water consumption as a competitive advantage for operators and investors. Surf parks represent a growing niche within the broader FEC and adventure leisure market worth watching for potential diversification.

articles.title: Frontgrid Expands ParadropVR to Third Sportainment Venue in Netherlands
articles.summary: Frontgrid's ParadropVR motion-based virtual reality flying experience is rolling out to a third Sparkx sportainment location, this time in the Netherlands, marking the attraction's first installation in the country. The Sparkx venue will host approximately 50 sport-related experiences blending physical challenges with immersive environments. This expansion highlights the growing viability of VR attractions in high-footfall, multi-experience venues relevant to FEC operators considering VR investments.

articles.title: Greenspan Projects Delivers Themed Adventure Golf for Europa-Park's Wild West Expansion
articles.summary: Greenspan Projects Ltd has completed Gold Rush Golf, a 12-hole interactive adventure golf course for Europa-Park's new Wild West-themed Silver Lake City expansion. The course incorporates interactive elements, photo opportunities, and immersive storytelling consistent with the park's broader themed environment. FEC operators and mini golf designers can look to this project as a benchmark for elevating adventure golf through narrative-driven design.

articles.title: How to Design a Dark Ride: Industry Leaders Share Key Engineering and Story Principles
articles.summary: Five attractions industry experts share insights on designing successful dark rides, emphasizing that story should lead technology and that early layout and building decisions are critical and difficult to reverse. Key takeaways include the importance of balancing budget, capacity, and immersion while planning for operations and maintenance from the start. FEC operators considering dark ride or immersive attraction investments will find this a valuable framework for evaluating supplier proposals.

articles.title: Six Flags Launches Hotel Partner Program for Seamless 'Stay & Play' Packages
articles.summary: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation has launched a new Hotel Partner Program allowing guests to bundle park admission with hotel accommodations through a streamlined booking platform. The initiative is aimed at increasing overnight stays and overall trip spending across its regional park portfolio. FEC operators near hotels or resorts may find inspiration in this partnership model for driving incremental revenue through accommodation tie-ins.

articles.title: Six Flags White Water Opens 42nd Season with Lazy River Renovation and Premium Upgrades
articles.summary: Six Flags White Water in Marietta, Georgia is opening for its 42nd season on May 9, highlighting a full renovation of the Little Hooch Lazy River and a new premium experience center alongside expanded food and beverage options. The investments reflect a broader industry trend of upgrading existing attractions rather than building new ones to improve guest experience and per-capita spending. Waterpark and FEC operators can take note of the premium experience center concept as a revenue diversification strategy.

articles.title: Legoland Resorts Launch New Indoor Space Coaster, Galacticoaster, Across Two Parks
articles.summary: LEGOLAND California and LEGOLAND Florida have debuted Galacticoaster, the resorts' first indoor space-themed family roller coaster, timed to National Space Day. The launch was amplified by a social marketing stunt sending real guest reaction videos airborne. For FEC operators, this launch illustrates how themed indoor coaster attractions can serve as anchor experiences for family-focused venues.

articles.title: Movie Park Germany Opens New Paramount Immersive Attraction for 30th Anniversary
articles.summary: Movie Park Germany has unveiled 'Journey to the Forbidden Chamber,' a new Paramount-licensed attraction opening as the park marks its 30th anniversary. The attraction represents a continued investment in IP-driven immersive experiences at regional theme parks. FEC operators considering licensed attraction partnerships can look to this as an example of leveraging entertainment IP to drive new visitation.

articles.title: Lake Compounce Marks 180th Anniversary Season with New Events and F&B Upgrades
articles.summary: Lake Compounce, America's oldest amusement park, is celebrating its 180th anniversary season opening May 9 with new events, entertainment programming, and upgraded food and beverage offerings. The milestone provides a strong marketing narrative for the park heading into summer. FEC operators can draw inspiration from how legacy venues use anniversary milestones to refresh guest interest and media coverage.

articles.title: Layered Reality Shuts Down Immersive Experience Venues After Parent Company Collapses
articles.summary: Layered Reality, operator of high-profile immersive experiences including Elvis Evolution and The War of the Worlds experience in London, has ceased trading after parent company Ellipsis Entertainment folded. Both venues are now closed, leaving staff and ticketholders affected. This closure is a cautionary tale for FEC operators and investors in the immersive entertainment space about the financial risks tied to single-IP experiential businesses.

articles.title: Hong Kong Disneyland Attendance Dips After Record Year, but Per-Capita Spending Hits Historic High
articles.summary: Hong Kong Disneyland reported attendance of 7.5 million visitors in fiscal 2025, down from a record 7.7 million the prior year when World of Frozen opened, with net profit also declining year-over-year. However, per-capita guest spending reached a record high, rising 2 percent. For FEC operators, this reinforces the growing importance of focusing on spend-per-visit strategies rather than purely attendance volume.

articles.title: Midland FEC Restaurant Secures Alcohol License, Expanding Revenue Options
articles.summary: A restaurant located inside an indoor amusement center in Midland has received an alcohol designation, allowing it to serve alcoholic beverages. Alcohol service is an increasingly common revenue driver for FEC operators looking to attract adult guests and boost food and beverage sales. This development reflects the broader trend of FECs evolving their F&B offerings to serve mixed adult and family audiences.

articles.title: Claw Arcade Facility Heads to City Planning Panel for Approval
articles.summary: A claw arcade facility is going before a city planning panel for approval, highlighting the regulatory steps involved in opening specialty arcade venues. The claw machine and prize redemption segment has seen significant growth driven by social media interest. FEC operators and new entrants should be aware of local zoning and planning requirements when adding or opening arcade-style attractions.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline Park Opens at Rivergate Bringing High-Energy Family Fun to Cape Town
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has opened a new high-energy family entertainment venue at Rivergate in Cape Town, South Africa, expanding the trampoline park concept into the African market. The opening reflects growing international demand for active family entertainment venues in emerging markets. FEC operators and franchisors looking at international expansion should note the appetite for trampoline park concepts in Africa.

articles.title: ISmash Rage Room Concept Coming to Rapid City in May
articles.summary: ISmash, a rage room and interactive smashing experience concept, is opening a new location in Rapid City in May, continuing the expansion of novelty entertainment attractions in secondary U.S. markets. Rage rooms have gained traction as a unique FEC attraction appealing to adults seeking stress-relief entertainment. Operators seeking to differentiate their attraction mix may find this a low-cost, high-interest addition to consider.

articles.title: Kidtopia Indoor Playground with Obstacle Courses and Climbing Opens in Dublin
articles.summary: Kidtopia, a new indoor playground concept featuring obstacle courses and indoor climbing, is coming to Dublin as part of the continued expansion of active play venues for children. The concept targets families with young children seeking structured indoor play environments. FEC operators focused on the younger demographic can look at Kidtopia as an example of purpose-built active play design.

articles.title: Dollywood Parks & Resorts Wins Seven Top Workplaces Culture Excellence Awards
articles.summary: Dollywood Parks & Resorts has earned seven awards in the 2026 Top Workplaces Culture Excellence and Industry Awards, recognizing the company's strong employee culture at its Smoky Mountain destination. In an industry facing persistent workforce challenges, Dollywood's recognition highlights the competitive advantage of investing in workplace culture. FEC operators struggling with staff retention and recruitment can look to Dollywood's approach as a model.

articles.title: Engineering Ethics in Attraction Design: Safety, Integrity, and Public Responsibility
articles.summary: A detailed overview of engineering ethics principles relevant to attraction and entertainment venue design emphasizes that safety is the highest obligation and that all decisions must prioritize public welfare over personal or financial gain. The piece references the National Society of Professional Engineers Code of Ethics as a foundational standard. FEC operators and their engineering partners should use this as a reference when evaluating design and construction decisions for new or renovated attractions.

articles.title: Amusement Center Agrees to $300K Settlement After Child Death Investigation
articles.summary: An amusement center has agreed to pay $300,000 following an investigation into the death of a young girl at its facility. The settlement highlights the severe legal and financial consequences operators face when safety incidents occur at FEC venues. Operators should treat this as a prompt to review liability coverage, safety training, and incident response protocols across all attractions.
